sessions.topic: Spacing token architecture — layer model definition
Token continuation — primitives, semantic model, export script
sessions.notes: Partial attendee list — Daniel and Rasmus not present. Full record unverified.

decisions.statement: Panda adopts a three-layer spacing token system: primitive (reference), semantic (system), and component.
decisions.context: Mirrors color token structure. Enables mode nesting per platform, theme, and accessibility without combinatorial explosion.

decisions.statement: The four-point grid is confirmed as the base unit for spacing (not eight-point).
The semantic layer is the primary design layer. Component tokens are allowed only for platform-specific exceptions, not as a general override mechanism.
decisions.context: Patrick's KISS position — component layer is a possibility, not a default.

decisions.statement: The semantic layer must remain finite and automated. Changes require cross-platform agreement via Slack or MR approval.
Shared vocabulary confirmed: 'reference' = primitive layer, 'system' = semantic layer, 'component' = component layer.
decisions.context: Proposed by Rasmus, confirmed by Facundo. All attendees aligned.

decisions.statement: Facundo and Justin will draft spacing token suggestions. iOS, Android, and web will each propose naming conventions. A common pattern will be extracted.
Four semantic spacing categories in scope: inset, stack, inline, and gutter. Names not locked — pending cross-platform input per DS-1-006.
Primitive token set defined and reviewed against scaled-up page examples (PDP, app, web).
decisions.context: ⚠️ Reconstructed from session summary — verify against full S2 record.

decisions.statement: Semantic layer = brands. Component layer = platforms. Cleaner mental model than Session 1 framing.
decisions.context: Significant architectural decision — Facundo ratification required. ⚠️ Reconstructed.

decisions.statement: Export script defined as required tooling for token delivery. Owner not yet assigned.
decisions.context: ⚠️ Reconstructed — verify full detail against S2 record.

decisions.statement: Value 44 status (spacing, size, or component token) discussed but not resolved.
decisions.context: ⚠️ Reconstructed — classification of 44 still open as OPN-2-003.

decisions.statement: The design system is treated as a design artifact first — design advances components without waiting for development sign-off.
decisions.context: Progress stalled requiring cross-team consensus. Design holds veto on system decisions.

decisions.statement: Design holds explicit veto power on design system decisions.
decisions.context: Developers remain to flag technical gaps but design decides.

decisions.statement: Sync meetings reduced to one representative per team plus Facundo. Patrick steps down; Daniel Wennberg remains as iOS rep.
decisions.context: Too many voices caused stalls. Pruned as of this session.

decisions.statement: A formal decision log is introduced to prevent reopening or overlapping past decisions.
decisions.context: Without a log, same discussions recurred with no retained resolution.

decisions.statement: The four-point grid mandate is reconfirmed. All spacing decisions follow it going forward.
decisions.context: Restatement of DS-1-002 as a formal design veto decision.

decisions.statement: Cross-platform token connection (Figma ↔ iOS ↔ Android automation) is temporarily deprioritized.
decisions.context: Overhead too high at current stage. Primitive layer stable enough to proceed.

decisions.statement: Immediate focus: finalize token layer — spacing, sizing, stroke width, and other numeric/string values.
decisions.context: Once tokens are finalized, component work expected to accelerate.

decisions.statement: The Claude project (panda-ds) is the official tool for sessions, decision log, and registry. Design = editor, developers = viewers/commenters.
Sync meetings continue bi-weekly. Meeting and channel ownership transfers to design team (Facundo).
Component workflow: finalize tokens → bind component to tokens → design review → hand off to development.
decisions.context: Prevents token decisions from being deferred until after component design.

decisions.statement: New components are planned as OKR candidates. Dev spends one OKR cycle implementing and fully adopting a component across all apps.
decisions.context: OKRs are a planning vehicle, not a time constraint.

decisions.statement: Figma versioning (Ready for Development frame saves a diffable version) must be actively communicated to all developers.
decisions.context: Knowledge gap — developers were not using this feature.

conflicts.previousDecision: Daniel: semantic layer reintroduces blast radius — shared tokens mean nobody changes them, same paralysis as today.
conflicts.currentDirection: Facundo + Patrick: component layer contains blast radius. Semantic stays clean if exceptions go to component tokens.
conflicts.implKeepOld: Semantic layer freezes in practice. Workarounds accumulate at component level anyway.
conflicts.implGoNew: Semantic layer stays clean and finite. Component exceptions stay scoped.
conflicts.notes: Daniel said 'let's go' but framed it as predicting what happens in a year — not a retraction. Must be validated with a live primitives+semantic example.

conflicts.topic: DS-2-002 naming sign-off — Daniel and Rasmus not present
conflicts.previousDecision: DS-1-006: naming process requires cross-platform input from iOS, Android, and web.
conflicts.currentDirection: DS-2-002 token names agreed in S2 without Daniel and Rasmus present. ⚠️ Reconstructed.
conflicts.implKeepOld: DS-2-002 names may need to be reopened if Daniel/Rasmus object.
conflicts.implGoNew: Names proceed as agreed — teams accept outcome of smaller session.
conflicts.notes: Needs explicit confirmation from Daniel and Rasmus before DS-2-002 is marked stable.

assumptions.statement: The semantic layer will remain small and stable once established, rarely requiring changes.
assumptions.risk: Automation breaks; governance overhead grows; platform adoption stalls.

assumptions.statement: Component tokens will be needed for only a small minority of exceptions (~0.5% per Rasmus's reference).
assumptions.risk: Component layer grows uncontrollably, reproducing the complexity it was meant to isolate.

assumptions.statement: Typography token mode-switching can serve as the working template for spacing token mode behavior.
assumptions.risk: Spacing modes behave differently — analogy breaks, layer model needs structural revision.

assumptions.statement: Tablet breakpoint support can be deferred without structural consequences.
assumptions.risk: Adding tablet later may require token renames or additional layer abstraction.

assumptions.statement: Once the token layer is finalized, component work will accelerate rapidly.
assumptions.risk: Token finalization may reveal additional unresolved questions (OPN-3-001, OPN-3-002) that re-introduce delays.

assumptions.statement: The primitive token layer will not change significantly after being locked.
assumptions.risk: Any change cascades into semantic and component tokens, invalidating downstream work.

assumptions.statement: Existing components can be retrofitted to the four-point grid without major rework.
assumptions.risk: Some components may require more effort, particularly where no Figma source exists (e.g. onboarding).

tokens.name: spacing.primitive.0
tokens.description: Zero spacing — explicit zero, not absence of token

tokens.name: spacing.primitive.2
tokens.description: Half-unit — use sparingly for micro adjustments only

tokens.name: spacing.primitive.4
tokens.description: Base grid unit (1×). Foundation of all spacing decisions per DS-1-002

tokens.name: spacing.primitive.8
tokens.description: 2× base unit

tokens.name: spacing.primitive.12
tokens.description: 3× base unit

tokens.name: spacing.primitive.16
tokens.description: 4× base unit

tokens.name: spacing.primitive.24
tokens.description: 6× base unit

tokens.name: spacing.primitive.32
tokens.description: 8× base unit

tokens.name: spacing.primitive.40
tokens.description: 10× base unit

tokens.name: spacing.primitive.48
tokens.description: 12× base unit

tokens.name: spacing.primitive.64
tokens.description: 16× base unit

tokens.name: spacing.primitive.80
tokens.description: 20× base unit

tokens.name: spacing.primitive.96
tokens.description: 24× base unit

tokens.name: spacing.primitive.128
tokens.description: 32× base unit — inclusion pending OPN-2-001

tokens.name: spacing.semantic.inset.*
tokens.description: Inset spacing — padding within contained elements. Naming pending OPN-1-001

tokens.name: spacing.semantic.stack.*
tokens.description: Stack spacing — vertical gap between stacked elements. Naming pending OPN-1-001

tokens.name: spacing.semantic.inline.*
tokens.description: Inline spacing — horizontal gap between inline elements. Naming pending OPN-1-001

tokens.name: spacing.semantic.gutter.*
tokens.description: Gutter spacing — layout-level column and row gaps. Naming pending OPN-1-001

tokens.name: color.*
tokens.description: Color token system — three-layer model assumed. Not yet in scope for this phase.

tokens.name: typography.*
tokens.description: Typography tokens — used as reference model for spacing mode behavior (ASM-1-003). Not yet documented.

tokens.name: sizing.*
tokens.description: Sizing tokens — stroke width, touch targets, icon sizes. Queued per DS-3-007. Open: minimum sizing OPN-3-001.

tokens.name: radius.*
tokens.description: Border radius tokens — not yet discussed.

tokens.name: motion.*
tokens.description: Motion and animation duration/easing tokens — not yet discussed.
